persona.tip: Start with who the AI should be: 'You are a Digital Project Manager at an aerospace company.'

task.tip: Name one specific action verb + deliverable: 'Create a 3-month AI Implementation & Adoption Strategy.'

context.tip: Add the details that matter: who it's for, constraints, and why (e.g. 'balancing speed, training and data-security compliance').

format.tip: Say how the output should look: 'Present it as a table with phases, owners and milestones.'

examples.tip: Show one example of what good output looks like — models imitate demonstrations better than descriptions.

tone.tip: State the voice: 'Use a professional, confident tone suitable for senior leadership.'

safeguards.tip: Add guardrails: 'Do not include confidential data; if unsure, say so rather than inventing details.'

flags.label: Vague wording
flags.advice: Replace vague words with specifics — name the system, audience, quantity, or deadline.

flags.label: Slang / informal
flags.advice: Models map slang poorly — write in plain, complete sentences.

flags.label: Conflicting instructions
flags.advice: Pick one: contradictory instructions confuse the model. Close one task before starting another.

flags.label: Biased framing
flags.advice: Remove assumptions about people or groups — ask for balanced, evidence-based output.

flags.label: Hard to parse
flags.advice: Use punctuation and capitalisation — a run-on wall of text degrades output quality.

fillers.pattern: in order to be able to
it would be great if you could

bestPractices.title: Concise and clear
bestPractices.detail: Every word should earn its place. Short, direct sentences beat padded politeness — the model doesn't need 'please kindly'.

bestPractices.title: Contextually relevant
bestPractices.detail: Include the details that change the answer: audience, constraints, domain, goal. Leave out everything else.

bestPractices.title: Aligned with the task
bestPractices.detail: One prompt, one job. Name the deliverable explicitly and close a task before starting a new one.

bestPractices.title: Show example demonstrations
bestPractices.detail: One good example beats three paragraphs of description — models imitate patterns.

bestPractices.title: Free from bias
bestPractices.detail: Don't bake assumptions about people or outcomes into the prompt; ask for balanced, evidence-based output.

bestPractices.title: Be specific, use keywords
bestPractices.detail: Specific nouns steer retrieval and attention: 'aircraft maintenance manuals', not 'the docs'.

bestPractices.title: Write legibly
bestPractices.detail: Correct punctuation, capitalisation, and grammar measurably improve output quality.

bestPractices.title: Ask for feedback
bestPractices.detail: End with: 'What questions do you have for me that would help you provide the best output?' — it surfaces your blind spots.

bestPractices.title: Break it up (chaining)
bestPractices.detail: Split complex workflows into a sequence of smaller prompts; feed each output into the next.

bestPractices.title: Iterative fine-tuning
bestPractices.detail: Don't expect perfection first try — nudge with follow-ups: 'Make the second paragraph shorter.'

bestPractices.title: Prompt triggers
bestPractices.detail: 'Let's think step-by-step' and similar phrases activate stronger reasoning patterns.

bestPractices.title: No slang or vagueness
bestPractices.detail: Slang and vague language produce low-quality or inaccurate responses — write plainly and precisely.

bestPractices.title: No conflicting instructions
bestPractices.detail: Contradictory or multi-topic requests confuse the model. One topic per conversation thread.

bestPractices.title: Delimit your structure
bestPractices.detail: Use ``` , """ or XML-style tags to separate instructions from data — the model can't mix up what's what.

bestPractices.title: Specify the output format
bestPractices.detail: Table? JSON? Bullet list with max 5 items? Say so — never make the model guess the shape.

bestPractices.title: Say do, not don't
bestPractices.detail: 'Write in active voice' works better than 'don't use passive voice' — models follow positive instructions more reliably.

bestPractices.title: Front-load key instructions
bestPractices.detail: Put role and task first, supporting detail after — early tokens anchor the model's plan.

bestPractices.title: Mind the token budget
bestPractices.detail: Tokens cost money, latency, and context space. Trim filler; prefer bullets over prose for lists; don't repeat instructions.
